facts.headline: India's most credit-stretched district
facts.lede: In Barmer, Rajasthan, banks have lent ₹340 for every ₹100 of deposits — the highest credit-deposit ratio in the country, far above the 60% RBI benchmark.

facts.headline: Kallakurichi: among India's heaviest borrowers
facts.lede: Banks in Kallakurichi, Tamil Nadu have lent ₹323 for every ₹100 they hold — placing it #2 in India for credit-to-deposit ratio.

facts.headline: Ariyalur: among India's heaviest borrowers
facts.lede: Banks in Ariyalur, Tamil Nadu have lent ₹288 for every ₹100 they hold — placing it #3 in India for credit-to-deposit ratio.

facts.headline: Suryapet: among India's heaviest borrowers
facts.lede: Banks in Suryapet, Telangana have lent ₹279 for every ₹100 they hold — placing it #4 in India for credit-to-deposit ratio.

facts.headline: Jangoan: among India's heaviest borrowers
facts.lede: Banks in Jangoan, Telangana have lent ₹277 for every ₹100 they hold — placing it #5 in India for credit-to-deposit ratio.

facts.headline: India's most savings-rich district
facts.lede: In Dibang Valley, Arunachal Pradesh, only ₹12 is lent for every ₹100 of deposits — the lowest credit-deposit ratio in the country. Local savers are funding lending elsewhere.

facts.headline: Shi Yomi: a deposit-rich, credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Just ₹12 of bank credit per ₹100 of deposits in Shi Yomi, Arunachal Pradesh — among India's lowest credit utilization rates.

facts.headline: Tawang: a deposit-rich, credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Just ₹14 of bank credit per ₹100 of deposits in Tawang, Arunachal Pradesh — among India's lowest credit utilization rates.

facts.headline: Upper Siang: a deposit-rich, credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Just ₹21 of bank credit per ₹100 of deposits in Upper Siang, Arunachal Pradesh — among India's lowest credit utilization rates.

facts.headline: West Singhbhum: a deposit-rich, credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Just ₹22 of bank credit per ₹100 of deposits in West Singhbhum, Jharkhand — among India's lowest credit utilization rates.

facts.headline: Barmer's lending suddenly jumped +216 percentage points
facts.lede: In a single quarter, Barmer (Rajasthan) saw its credit-deposit ratio rocket from 124% to 340% — one of the sharpest single-quarter expansions in the data. Likely a large industrial loan disbursement.

facts.headline: Kra Daadi's lending suddenly jumped +213 percentage points
facts.lede: In a single quarter, Kra Daadi (Arunachal Pradesh) saw its credit-deposit ratio rocket from 45% to 258% — one of the sharpest single-quarter expansions in the data. Likely a large industrial loan disbursement.

facts.headline: Chengalpattu's lending suddenly jumped +151 percentage points
facts.lede: In a single quarter, Chengalpattu (Tamil Nadu) saw its credit-deposit ratio rocket from 90% to 241% — one of the sharpest single-quarter expansions in the data. Likely a large industrial loan disbursement.

facts.headline: Niuland's lending suddenly jumped +145 percentage points
facts.lede: In a single quarter, Niuland (Nagaland) saw its credit-deposit ratio rocket from 48% to 193% — one of the sharpest single-quarter expansions in the data. Likely a large industrial loan disbursement.

facts.headline: Chennai's lending suddenly jumped +121 percentage points
facts.lede: In a single quarter, Chennai (Tamil Nadu) saw its credit-deposit ratio rocket from 110% to 231% — one of the sharpest single-quarter expansions in the data. Likely a large industrial loan disbursement.

facts.headline: India's most banked district
facts.lede: PUNE (Maharashtra) has 18,502 banking outlets — branches, ATMs, BCs and CSPs combined. Each is mapped to GPS coordinates in RBI's DBIE database.

facts.headline: Where every bank touchpoint is rural
facts.lede: In BAGESHWAR, Uttarakhand, 100% of all banking outlets are in rural centres (population <10,000) — the most rural-skewed district in India with meaningful banking presence.

facts.headline: India's BC capital
facts.lede: MURSHIDABAD (West Bengal) hosts 14,236 Business Correspondents — agents who bring banking to villages without branches. The largest BC network in any single district.

facts.headline: Aadhaar's busiest enrollment district
facts.lede: In just one quarter (2025-12), 24 Paraganas South, West-Bengal processed 24,083 new Aadhaar enrollments — more than any other district in India.

facts.headline: Where children dominate Aadhaar enrollment
facts.lede: In Nawada, Bihar, 79% of new Aadhaar enrollments are aged 5–17 — a youth-heavy enrollment surge driven by school-age catch-up.

facts.headline: India's wealthiest district by Meta's RWI
facts.lede: Central, Delhi, has the highest mean Relative Wealth Index in India — +1.26 on Meta's satellite-and-connectivity-derived wealth scale (calibrated against DHS surveys).
facts.source: SHRUG v2.1 (Development Data Lab) — Meta RWI 2021

facts.headline: India's most economically vulnerable district by RWI
facts.lede: By Meta's Relative Wealth Index, Bijapur (Chhattisgarh) is the poorest district in India — RWI -0.53, two full standard deviations below the wealthiest.

facts.headline: India's most unequal district
facts.lede: Bikaner, Rajasthan, has the largest within-district wealth gap in India — the richest 2.4 km cell scores +1.80 on Meta's RWI while the poorest scores -1.37, a spread of 3.17.

facts.headline: India's fastest-electrifying district (by satellite)
facts.lede: Sheohar, Bihar got brighter at night by 2902% between 2012 and 2023 — the largest growth in nighttime light intensity of any Indian district. A proxy for new infrastructure, urbanization, and economic activity.

facts.headline: India's brightest spot from space
facts.lede: Bathinda, Punjab contains the brightest nighttime cell in India — peak radiance 917 nW/cm²/sr in 2023. Typically the largest urban core.

facts.headline: India's most-connected rural district
facts.lede: By 2015, Surguja (Chhattisgarh) had built 9,991 km of PMGSY rural roads — the longest cumulative network in any single district from the central rural connectivity scheme.

facts.headline: India's most expensive rural-road district
facts.lede: PMGSY roads in Kupwara, Jammu and Kashmir cost ₹75 lakh per km on average — the highest cost-per-km of any well-served district. Likely reflects difficult terrain (hills, forests, river crossings).

facts.headline: India's UPI capital
facts.lede: In just one quarter (2025-03), Bengaluru Urban (Karnataka) saw 167.4 crore PhonePe UPI transactions worth ₹2 crore — the largest single-district UPI volume in the country.

facts.headline: Telangana: India's highest-lending state
facts.lede: The median district in Telangana has a credit-deposit ratio of 184% — the highest of any major Indian state. Driven by intensive agricultural credit and microfinance.

facts.headline: Uttarakhand: most savings-rich state
facts.lede: In Uttarakhand, the median district has a credit-deposit ratio of just 34% — the lowest of any major state. Local deposits flow into lending elsewhere in India.

facts.headline: India's NE region is structurally credit-starved
facts.lede: Across all 8 Northeast states, the median district has a credit-deposit ratio of just 71% — compared to 83% in the rest of India. Reflects collateral gaps, terrain, and low credit demand in remote economies.
facts.stat: NE median CDR 71% vs rest of India 83%
facts.source: SLBC quarterly data, latest available across 119 NE + 386 non-NE districts

facts.headline: Rajasthan's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Barmer leads Rajasthan on credit-deposit ratio at 340% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Tamil Nadu's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Kallakurichi leads Tamil Nadu on credit-deposit ratio at 323% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Telangana's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Suryapet leads Telangana on credit-deposit ratio at 279% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Andhra Pradesh's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Palnadu leads Andhra Pradesh on credit-deposit ratio at 250% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Nagaland's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Longleng leads Nagaland on credit-deposit ratio at 226% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Odisha's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Jharsuguda leads Odisha on credit-deposit ratio at 184% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Manipur's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Thoubal leads Manipur on credit-deposit ratio at 184% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Gujarat's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Morbi leads Gujarat on credit-deposit ratio at 180% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Haryana's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Panipat leads Haryana on credit-deposit ratio at 170% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Karnataka's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Haveri leads Karnataka on credit-deposit ratio at 154% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Maharashtra's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Mumbai leads Maharashtra on credit-deposit ratio at 143% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Chhattisgarh's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Raipur leads Chhattisgarh on credit-deposit ratio at 129% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Mizoram's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Lawngtlai leads Mizoram on credit-deposit ratio at 121% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Meghalaya's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Ri Bhoi leads Meghalaya on credit-deposit ratio at 120% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Assam's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: West Karbi Anglong leads Assam on credit-deposit ratio at 111% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: Uttarakhand's heaviest-borrowing district
facts.lede: Udam Singh Nagar leads Uttarakhand on credit-deposit ratio at 100% — banks have loaned out far more than they collect locally.

facts.headline: India's largest Jan Dhan district
facts.lede: 24 Paraganas South, West-Bengal has 56.2 lakh PM Jan Dhan accounts — the most of any single district. Each is a zero-balance basic savings account opened under India's flagship financial-inclusion scheme.

facts.headline: Where Jan Dhan is overwhelmingly women's banking
facts.lede: In Paschim Bardhaman, West-Bengal, 65% of PMJDY accounts are held by women — the highest female share of any major district. Jan Dhan has been India's largest women's financial-inclusion program.

facts.headline: Inclusion on paper, not in practice
facts.lede: In Lunglei, Mizoram, 25% of all Jan Dhan accounts have a zero balance — never used after opening. The highest dormancy rate among major Jan Dhan districts.

facts.headline: India's biggest farmer-credit district
facts.lede: Hooghly, West-Bengal has 4.0 lakh active Kisan Credit Cards — the most of any district. Each card provides revolving short-term credit at subsidized rates for crop production and allied activities.

facts.headline: India's largest agricultural credit book
facts.lede: In Purba Bardhaman, West-Bengal, KCC outstanding amounts to ₹21 crore — the largest farmer-credit book of any district. Crops include cotton, paddy, sugarcane.

facts.headline: India's investment-services capital
facts.lede: MUMBAI SUBURBAN, Maharashtra hosts 15,310 capital-market access points — depository participants, mutual fund distributors, and service centres. The deepest investor-facing infrastructure in India.

facts.headline: India's mutual-fund-distributor density leader
facts.lede: MUMBAI SUBURBAN, Maharashtra has 11,626 individual Mutual Fund Distributors registered with AMFI — the highest concentration in India. Each is a licensed advisor/seller of mutual fund products.

facts.headline: India's investment desert
facts.lede: In MIRPUR, Jammu And Kashmir, only 0 capital-market access points exist — virtually no infrastructure for opening a Demat account or buying mutual funds without travelling out of the district.

facts.headline: India's only districts that got darker since 2012
facts.lede: North, Delhi is one of the rare Indian districts where mean nighttime light intensity actually decreased between 2012 and 2023, dropping 0%. Likely reflects deurbanization or measurement-area changes.

facts.headline: Siwan got 5x brighter in a decade
facts.lede: Nighttime light over Siwan, Bihar grew 1835% between 2012 and 2023 — proof of dramatic infrastructure investment, electrification, and urbanization.

facts.headline: India's biggest aggregate light footprint
facts.lede: Pune, Maharashtra contributes the largest total nighttime-light output of any Indian district — a proxy for total economic activity in absolute terms.

facts.headline: India's busiest rural-road builder
facts.lede: By 2015, Surguja, Chhattisgarh had built 1,152 separate PMGSY road segments — more than any other district. The flagship rural connectivity scheme launched in 2000.

facts.headline: India's most cost-efficient rural-road program
facts.lede: PMGSY built 221 km of rural roads in Kurung Kumey, Arunachal Pradesh at just ₹10 lakh per km — the lowest cost-per-km of any well-served district. Likely flat plains terrain.

facts.headline: Adult Aadhaar catch-up surge
facts.lede: In Shivpuri, Madhya-Pradesh, 10% of new enrollments are adults aged 18+. The largest adult-share among major-volume districts — likely catching up undocumented older residents.

facts.headline: Where Aadhaar enrollment is mostly newborns
facts.lede: In Visakhapatanam, Andhra-Pradesh, 95% of all new Aadhaar enrollments are under age 5 — the highest share of any major-volume district. Reflects newborn registration linked to vaccination/welfare programs.

facts.headline: Nanded just crossed the 100% credit-deposit threshold
facts.lede: After years below the line, Nanded (Maharashtra) became a 'lending-out' district in 2025-12 — banks now lend more than they collect. Its CDR moved from 97% to 101%.

facts.headline: Karauli just crossed the 100% credit-deposit threshold
facts.lede: After years below the line, Karauli (Rajasthan) became a 'lending-out' district in 2025-12 — banks now lend more than they collect. Its CDR moved from 70% to 117%.

facts.headline: Coochbehar just crossed the 100% credit-deposit threshold
facts.lede: After years below the line, Coochbehar (West Bengal) became a 'lending-out' district in 2025-03 — banks now lend more than they collect. Its CDR moved from 80% to 106%.

facts.headline: A banking footprint that hasn't translated to wealth
facts.lede: Bijapur, Chhattisgarh has 599 banking outlets but a Meta RWI of -0.53 — well below the national average. A district where banking presence outpaces general prosperity.

facts.headline: Aadhaar enrollment is exploding in Saran
facts.lede: Between 2025-06 and 2025-12, Saran (Bihar) saw new Aadhaar enrollments climb from 1k to 14k per quarter — up 865%.

facts.headline: India's biggest district deposit base
facts.lede: Banks hold ₹17.9 lakh crore in deposits in Mumbai, Maharashtra — the largest district-level deposit base in our SLBC data. A reflection of urban concentration and savings flows.

facts.headline: #2 most deposit-rich district: Mumbai Suburban
facts.lede: Mumbai Suburban, Maharashtra ranks #2 in India by total district-level bank deposits — ₹10.4 lakh crore held across all branches. Reveals where India's savings concentrate.

facts.headline: #3 most deposit-rich district: Pune
facts.lede: Pune, Maharashtra ranks #3 in India by total district-level bank deposits — ₹6.2 lakh crore held across all branches. Reveals where India's savings concentrate.

facts.headline: #4 most deposit-rich district: Hyderabad
facts.lede: Hyderabad, Telangana ranks #4 in India by total district-level bank deposits — ₹4.2 lakh crore held across all branches. Reveals where India's savings concentrate.

facts.headline: #5 most deposit-rich district: Thane
facts.lede: Thane, Maharashtra ranks #5 in India by total district-level bank deposits — ₹3.5 lakh crore held across all branches. Reveals where India's savings concentrate.

facts.headline: India's biggest health-insurance gain (2016→2021)
facts.lede: In just 5 years between NFHS-4 and NFHS-5, household health insurance coverage in Barmer (rajasthan) jumped from 9% to 98% — a +89-point increase. The largest gain in any Indian district.

facts.headline: Dungarpur: a 5-year insurance miracle
facts.lede: Health-insurance coverage in Dungarpur (rajasthan) more than doubled between NFHS-4 and NFHS-5, climbing from 10% to 98% of households — likely from state-funded health programs reaching new households.

facts.headline: Anjaw: rare insurance backslide
facts.lede: Health-insurance coverage in Anjaw (arunachal-pradesh) actually fell between NFHS-4 (2016) and NFHS-5 (2021) — from 75% to 18%. One of the few districts to lose ground over the decade.

facts.headline: India's median health-insurance coverage doubled in 5 years
facts.lede: Across 634 districts surveyed in both NFHS-4 (2016) and NFHS-5 (2021), the average household coverage rate jumped from 21% to 41%. A massive expansion driven by state-led schemes like Ayushman Bharat-PMJAY.

facts.headline: Delhi: India's most lit-up state
facts.lede: By 2023 average nighttime-light intensity, Delhi's districts shine the brightest among major states. Density of urbanization and industrial activity at scale.

facts.headline: Arunachal Pradesh: India's darkest state from above
facts.lede: Arunachal Pradesh has the lowest average district nightlight intensity in India — 0.06 on the VIIRS scale. Reflects a combination of low population density, mountainous terrain, and limited industrial activity.

facts.headline: Bihar: India's fastest-electrifying state
facts.lede: Between 2012 and 2023, Bihar's average district nightlight intensity grew 477% — the largest decadal expansion among major Indian states. Captures a generational infrastructure build-out.

facts.headline: West-Bengal: India's biggest Jan Dhan state
facts.lede: With 3.7 crore active PMJDY accounts across its districts, West-Bengal has the largest Jan Dhan footprint of any Indian state in our data.

facts.headline: Chandel: a sustained credit slowdown
facts.lede: Over the last two years (since 2024-03), Chandel's credit-deposit ratio fell from 144% to 75% — one of the most sustained credit shrinkages in our data. Local lending has cooled while deposits hold steady.

facts.headline: Korea: bucking the lending boom
facts.lede: While most Indian districts saw credit grow over 2022–2024, Korea (Chhattisgarh) saw its CDR fall from 82% to 38%.

facts.headline: Uttar-Pradesh: India's Aadhaar enrollment leader
facts.lede: In just one quarter (2025-12), Uttar-Pradesh processed 4.2 lakh new Aadhaar enrollments — more than any other state. Reflects ongoing catch-up registration.
facts.stat: 4.2 lakh new enrollments in one quarter

facts.headline: India's most metropolitan banking footprint
facts.lede: In Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka, 80% of all banking outlets sit in metropolitan centres (population >10 lakh) — the most urban-concentrated district network in India.

facts.headline: Maharashtra: India's top deposit state
facts.lede: Across all districts in Maharashtra for which we have SLBC data, banks hold ₹48.1 lakh crore in deposits — the largest aggregated deposit base of any state. Reflects industrial wealth + savings concentration.

facts.headline: India's quietest district from space
facts.lede: Dibang Valley, Arunachal Pradesh is India's darkest district by night even in 2023 — mean nightlight intensity of just 0.002, far below national average. A signature of remoteness and low population density.

facts.headline: Maharashtra: India's UPI superstate
facts.lede: In a single quarter, Maharashtra's residents made 363.4 crore PhonePe UPI transactions — the highest of any state. India runs on QR-code payments now.

facts.headline: India's NE has 3.4% of the nation's banking outlets
facts.lede: Across all 8 Northeast states, RBI counts 84,189 banking outlets — just 3.4% of India's total 2,472,495. The NE is home to ~3.8% of India's population, so banking density per capita is roughly comparable, but the absolute footprint is small.

facts.headline: Arunachal Pradesh's most credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Of all districts in Arunachal Pradesh, Dibang Valley has the lowest credit-deposit ratio at 12% — savings substantially exceed local lending here.

facts.headline: Jharkhand's most credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Of all districts in Jharkhand, West Singhbhum has the lowest credit-deposit ratio at 22% — savings substantially exceed local lending here.

facts.headline: Gujarat's most credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Of all districts in Gujarat, Dang has the lowest credit-deposit ratio at 23% — savings substantially exceed local lending here.

facts.headline: Uttarakhand's most credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Of all districts in Uttarakhand, Bageshwar has the lowest credit-deposit ratio at 24% — savings substantially exceed local lending here.

facts.headline: Meghalaya's most credit-starved corner
facts.lede: Of all districts in Meghalaya, East Jaintia Hills has the lowest credit-deposit ratio at 25% — savings substantially exceed local lending here.

facts.headline: Bihar's top-lending district
facts.lede: In Bihar, Purnia leads on credit-deposit ratio at 97% — banks lending more aggressively here than in any other district of the state.

facts.headline: Tripura's top-lending district
facts.lede: In Tripura, Dhalai leads on credit-deposit ratio at 91% — banks lending more aggressively here than in any other district of the state.

facts.headline: West Bengal's top-lending district
facts.lede: In West Bengal, Kolkata leads on credit-deposit ratio at 87% — banks lending more aggressively here than in any other district of the state.

facts.headline: Arunachal Pradesh's top-lending district
facts.lede: In Arunachal Pradesh, Kra Daadi leads on credit-deposit ratio at 80% — banks lending more aggressively here than in any other district of the state.

facts.headline: India enrolled 23.5 lakh new Aadhaars in one quarter
facts.lede: In just 2025-12 alone, India processed 23.5 lakh fresh Aadhaar enrollments across 714 districts. The world's largest biometric ID system continues to expand.
facts.stat: 23.5 lakh new enrollments in one quarter

facts.headline: India's biggest Jan Dhan district has 14× the median
facts.lede: 24 Paraganas South has 56.2 lakh PMJDY accounts while the median Indian district has only 4.0 lakh — a 14-fold gap that reveals how concentrated Jan Dhan deposits are in a few large districts.

facts.headline: India's banking footprint: 2.47M outlets
facts.lede: RBI's DBIE database catalogues 2,472,495 banking outlets across the country — branches, BCs, ATMs, CSPs and DBUs combined. Each one geo-tagged.

facts.headline: India's total night-time light grew 97% in a decade
facts.lede: Adding up nighttime-light radiance across all Indian districts, India shone 97% brighter in 2023 than in 2012. A satellite signature of the country's economic growth.

facts.headline: India's richest-poorest gap: 1.8 RWI points
facts.lede: Between India's wealthiest district (Central, Delhi, RWI +1.26) and its poorest (Bijapur, Chhattisgarh, RWI -0.53), the wealth gap is 1.8 units on Meta's index — roughly 2 standard deviations.
